title: ‘Furiosa’ Is Here! Witness It!
format: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins review George Miller's 'Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga,' work through the best prequels ever made, rank the five Mad Max films, and handicap its box office and Oscar chances.

segments.heading: 'Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga'
segments.note: a real achievement that stops short of great

films.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
films.note: George Miller's fifth Mad Max film, a prequel tracing how the girl is taken from the Green Place to the Citadel. Anya Taylor-Joy plays the older Furiosa (Charlize Theron's role in Fury Road) and Alyla Browne the child; Chris Hemsworth's talky warlord Dementus and Tom Burke's Praetorian Jack anchor a story built around four set pieces. Sean calls it 'really, really, really good' but not great: an automatic 8 out of 10 for the Gastown sniper sequence alone, though he thinks it's too iterative on Fury Road, bloated at 2 hours 25 (the longest Mad Max), and hobbled by an ending that cuts to Fury Road footage. Both agree Hemsworth's comic register deflates the movie's doom-laden stakes. Amanda's final split: nine out of ten as filmmaking, seven as a moviegoing experience.

segments.heading: George Miller, his cast, and his crew

films.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
films.note: The touchstone the whole episode measures against. Sean puts it in the lineage of the Jazz Singer and Lawrence of Arabia, a 'movies can do that' film and among the best of the 21st century; Furiosa is structurally and visually so similar that he can't see how anyone calls it better.

films.title: Three Thousand Years of Longing
films.note: Sean's recommended key to Furiosa: the same register of affection for ancient tales and recurring myth, even if he wasn't a big fan of it.

films.title: Babe
films.note: Cited as another Miller treatise on old stories that recur over time; Amanda's 'beloved' entry in his filmography.

films.title: Happy Feet
films.note: Sean's example of Miller being willing to try things that shouldn't work: a movie he never expected to enjoy and did.

films.title: The Witches of Eastwick
films.note: Filed under Miller's crazy-stuff-works-best tendency: 'a blast and really fun, but also kind of a mess.'

films.title: Lorenzo's Oil
films.note: 'Kind of beautiful, but also doesn't work at times': proof for Sean that Miller is a messy filmmaker by design.

films.title: The Fall Guy
films.note: Amanda enjoyed it more than Furiosa and says the two are 'two sides of the same coin'; its deepfake plot primed her to notice the digital work on Alyla Browne's face.

films.title: Anyone But You
films.note: Where Charlee Fraser (who plays Furiosa's mother chasing the abductors on a motorcycle) last turned up, as Glenn Powell's Australian ex.

films.title: The Souvenir
films.note: Amanda's 'beloved' source for Tom Burke, who plays rig driver Praetorian Jack, the template for the Furiosa look.

films.title: Witness
films.note: One of DP John Seale's credits invoked as an 'Amanda DP'; Seale, in his 80s, effectively retired after Fury Road, so Simon Duggan shot Furiosa instead.

films.title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
films.note: Named alongside Witness and The English Patient as the John Seale résumé.

films.title: The English Patient
films.note: The film that won Seale his Academy Award for cinematography.

films.title: Spiderhead
films.note: The closest Hemsworth has come to a heavy before this, though Sean notes he's still doing the smug wisecracking Thor routine there.

films.title: Star Trek
films.note: J.J. Abrams' reboot, where Hemsworth plays George Kirk, James T. Kirk's father; the hosts agree the first is the best of that run.

segments.heading: The best prequels of all time

films.title: The Godfather Part II
films.note: The arguable American classic that's only 'sort of a prequel': a Michael Corleone continuation braided with young Vito in Italy.

films.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
films.note: A prequel almost by accident: you only realize it when the Man with No Name picks up the outfit he wears in the first two films.

films.title: Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me
films.note: Sean calls it one of the best movies of all time; Amanda, less sold on the Lynch heads, needles that Lynch 'has nothing to say' before conceding he's wonderful.

films.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
films.note: Amanda likes it but has 'absolutely no memory' of Darth Vader showing up; Sean recommends watching it on YouTube high, laughing maniacally.

films.title: Prometheus
films.note: A quick 'very, very, very good film' from Amanda when it comes up on the list.

films.title: Pearl
films.note: Ti West's Mia Goth prequel to X; Amanda got to it before MaXXXine, which she's very excited about.

films.title: MaXXXine
films.note: The next chapter Amanda is anticipating; Sean has bundled the Longlegs trailer onto her Maxine episode for 'a double whammy of pain.'

films.title: Longlegs
films.note: Whose trailer Amanda hadn't watched yet, added to her Maxine coverage.

films.title: X-Men: First Class
films.note: Amanda's verdict: 'A-class.' She loves this one.

films.title: Rise of the Planet of the Apes
films.note: Noted as a prequel they'd discussed recently.

films.title: Fast Five
films.note: Someone pointed out it's technically a prequel; Sean doesn't respect the classification and takes a shot at Vin Diesel.

segments.heading: The Mad Max Movie Rankings
segments.note: Amanda's quick order: full rankings next week with Chris and Joanna
segments.subject: Mad Max

films.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
films.note: No. 1 for both, straightforwardly the best of the five.

films.title: Mad Max 2
films.note: The Road Warrior lands at No. 2, the pure road-war movie whose oil-rig chase Furiosa homages.

films.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
films.note: Slots in at No. 3 on a single viewing.

films.title: Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome
films.note: No. 4. Amanda insists nobody puts enough respect on Tina Turner's name.

films.title: Mad Max
films.note: Last for Amanda: significant as the franchise starter but the oldest and lowest-budget, a 70s revenge movie.

segments.heading: Box office and awards
segments.note: a soft market and a thin Best Picture field

films.title: Dune: Part Two
films.note: The one big, noisy movie both feel confident is a Best Picture lock in a weak year.

films.title: Dune
films.note: Grouped with its sequel as movies that, like the Mad Max films, take their stakes seriously against 20 years of winking superhero fare.

films.title: Kingdom of the Planet of the Apes
films.note: Sean's comparison point: not that good, but it met modest expectations. The kind of franchise movie the box office needs right now.

films.title: Black Adam
films.note: The kind of franchise product Furiosa and Kingdom are an improved version of.

films.title: The Garfield Movie
films.note: The weekend's other new release; Sean skipped his Saturday screening for nap time and figures Chris Pratt's cat could win the weekend over Hemsworth.

films.title: Joker
films.note: Invoked as a comparable 'we've never seen that' origin-story phenomenon Furiosa can't replicate.

films.title: Anora
films.note: Sean Baker's film premiered at Cannes that day to raves, one of the few genuinely positive festival reactions.

films.title: Challengers
films.note: Amanda loves it; cited amid a general wish for new energy at the movies.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: Sean's shorthand for Fury Road's 2015 awards run: ten nominations, six wins, dominant below the line, but no directing or picture win.

referenced.group: The Linklater and Glen Powell setup

The Good, the Bad and the Ugly.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly

Kingdom of the Planet of the Apes.title: Kingdom of the Planet of the Apes
